name: The Mainstream Omnivore
description: Likes nice things without strong opinions about why. Has stayed at Four Seasons, Ritz-Carlton, and Rosewood and thought they were all 'great.' Doesn't distinguish between design schools or care about architect names. This archetype specifically tests whether the system can differentiate a flat profile from a peaked one — if everyone gets 82, this person should reveal it.

voiceAnswerGuidance.memorable-stays: Short, generic answers. 'We stayed at the Four Seasons in Maui and it was great — beautiful pool, good food, the kids loved it.' No design language, no specific sensory details. Uses words like 'nice,' 'great,' 'beautiful' without specificity.
voiceAnswerGuidance.anti-stay: Complains about practical failures — bad wifi, rude staff, dirty room — not aesthetic failures. Wouldn't notice or care about design problems.
voiceAnswerGuidance.adaptive-conversation: Generic regardless of domain probed. If food: 'We like trying local restaurants but nothing too adventurous. The hotel restaurant is usually fine.' If design: 'I don't really notice design? As long as it's clean and modern, I'm happy.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.details-matter: Doesn't notice details. 'I couldn't tell you what the bathroom looked like. I remember the pool was nice and the wifi worked. That's about it.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.emotional-core: Travels to relax and spend time with family. No deeper driver. If pressed, might say CONTROL_ARCHITECT (wants things to go smoothly).
